dependsOn: Remove cosmetic anti-copy shields (keydown/contextmenu blockers, pointer-events:none, image oncontextmenu)

orderedSlices.slice: Client-side performance: lazy-load Pyodide/CodeMirror/confetti, defer scripts, preconnect, move icons CSS to head, image dimensions + lazy-loading
orderedSlices.rationale: The biggest INP/LCP win: stop eagerly booting the Pyodide WASM VM in DOMContentLoaded and trigger it on first user intent (IntersectionObserver / first editor click) (PERF-05); add defer to bootstrap/flatpickr/CodeMirror (PERF-06), preconnect the three CDNs (PERF-07), move bootstrap-icons <link> into <head> (PERF-08), trim the Google Fonts request (PERF-09). Add explicit width/height + aspect-ratio and loading=lazy/decoding=async to calendar/home images (PERF-01/02) — the pure-markup half that does not need the server. Sequenced after a11y because the interpreter/editor DOM and the calendar img tags are the very elements a11y just modified; batching JS-init + attribute changes here avoids reopening challenge_detail.html and challenges.html a third time. The WebP-derivative half of the image story (PERF-03) is server-only and deferred.
dependsOn: Make core interactions keyboard/SR operable: calendar tiles, emoji ratings, lightbox dialog, nav/register/validation ARIA, contrast fixes

orderedSlices.slice: Fluid/mobile-first responsiveness: clamp() typography, calendar grid, interpreter heights, modal chrome, code overflow, replace .w-lg-50
orderedSlices.rationale: Responsiveness is intentionally near-last because it depends on BOTH the token layer (it edits sizes/radii that should reference tokens where they exist) AND the a11y/perf DOM changes (fluid interpreter heights apply to the now lazy-initialized CodeMirror; modal clamp() applies to the now-dialog lightbox). Introduce clamp() body/heading type in style.css (R-02), fluid calendar cell sizing + overflow-x fallback (R-01), mobile-first interpreter heights (R-03), fluid modal offsets/padding (R-05), the standalone code-block overflow-x guard (R-06), emoji-row flex-wrap + fluid gap (R-07), Flatpickr wrapper (R-08), footer (R-09), and replace the page-local .w-lg-50 hack with Bootstrap grid or intrinsic flex (R-04). Doing this after tokens+a11y+perf means each affected line in challenges.html/challenge_detail.html/style.css is touched in its final form once. Container queries (R-10) are forward-looking, lowest priority.
dependsOn: Client-side performance: lazy-load Pyodide/CodeMirror/confetti, defer scripts, preconnect, move icons CSS to head, image dimensions + lazy-loading
orderedSlices.methodology: Marcotte mobile-first fluid design + CSS Grid/Flex best practice (clamp/minmax)

orderedSlices.rationale: With logic, UI, and lineage aligned, wire the connective tissue that reaches the server via auto-deploy and needs no live host to be correct in code: add GET /health (liveness) and GET /readiness (SELECT 1) routes (SH-1), introduce a leveled logger replacing print() (SH-2), add retry-with-backoff Session for FCC/quote fetches (SH-3), stop swallowing failures / make /api/rate report notified:bool and add JSON error envelope + errorhandlers (SH-4/SH-9), and guard home()'s full DB-fetch block against a 500 (SH-5). This closes the logical loop the KG describes (calendar -> /api/challenge single source of truth already exists; now every route degrades and reports truthfully). Sequenced here because it is app.py-centric again and benefits from the slice-2 helper consolidation; it also defines the /readiness endpoint that the server-only watchdog rewiring will later consume.
dependsOn: DRY consolidation of markdown rendering, FCC-quote fetch, and business rules into helpers/model properties
orderedSlices.methodology: Observability + Graceful Degradation + Retry/Circuit-Breaker (SRE golden signals, 12-Factor XI, Nygard)

orderedSlices.slice: Code-level security hardening that reaches the server via auto-deploy (auth-gate /admin, fail-closed SECRET_KEY, debug off, deterministic image filenames, model/DB honesty)
orderedSlices.rationale: The code-side of the security posture, safe to push now (owner defers actual secret ROTATION to production): add @admin_required gating both GET/POST of /admin via a server-side ADMIN_KEY compare since OTP/OAuth are stubs (SU-6/S-4), make SECRET_KEY fail-closed with a shipped .env.example (SU-1/S-8), guard app.run debug via FLASK_DEBUG defaulting False (SU-2/S-7), make image filenames a deterministic function of date_id to stop orphan twins (SSOT-2) and align the model to the live NOT NULL image_path contract with an explicit empty-string sentinel (SSOT-5/DBN-3). These are code edits that harden behavior the moment they deploy, without needing the unreachable host. Placed after self-healing so /admin auth and error handling are consistent with the new errorhandlers.

deferredServerItems: DBN-1/DBN-2/DBN-6 (schema-reconciling migration): author ONE additive idempotent Alembic migration (down_revision=49b711d2aba8) that guarded-adds challenges.challenge_number/fcc_*/source and guarded-creates users/comments/concept_strengths/user_notebooks, plus a nullable-safe UNIQUE on challenge_number; downgrade=no-op (append-only). Then retire db.create_all() as a schema-evolution mechanism and the create_*_tables.py scripts. Requires running flask db upgrade against the live SQLite on 192.168.1.150.

PERF-03 (image derivatives): extend image_processor.py to emit 300x400 + 800px WebP variants and serve via <picture>/srcset. Requires server-side regeneration of 150 images and OpenCV/Pillow on the host.
PERF-04 (cache/compression): far-future immutable Cache-Control for /static/images + gzip/brotli via nginx or Flask-Compress. Requires nginx/gunicorn config on the host.

PERF-10/PERF-11/PERF-12 (server compute): precompute markdown HTML at write time into nullable columns (append-only), single-pass calendar month build, move the synchronous 5s FCC quote fetch off the home request path into cron/background. Depends on the reconciling migration for the new nullable columns.

SU-2 runtime (gunicorn + systemd): author/commit deploy/portfolio.service running gunicorn (Restart=always) and remove auto_deploy.sh's nohup python app.py debug-server fallback. Requires the live host to install/activate the unit.

SU-3 (sudoers): delete /etc/sudoers.d/90-nishan-overrides NOPASSWD:ALL, retire fix_sudo.py/server_maintenance.py sudoers writes, keep only the scoped restart rule, validate with visudo -c / sudo -l. Host-only.

SU-4/SU-5 (supply chain): gate auto_deploy ff-merge on git verify-commit/signed-tag; sparse-checkout to keep the paramiko/RCE ops scripts and telegram_bridge.py out of the deployed tree; git filter-repo to purge hardcoded secrets from history. Host + repo-history operation.
SU-9 (privilege separation): run the deploy timer as a dedicated unprivileged user distinct from the bot user; make the checkout non-writable by the runtime user. Host-only.

S-1/S-2 secret rotation (owner-deferred to production): rotate the SSH password, revoke/reissue the Telegram BotFather token, move to server-side .env. Explicitly deferred by owner to the production window.

SH-6/SH-7/SH-8/SH-10/SH-11 (server-side resilience): repoint network_watchdog.sh at /readiness with retry/cooldown and post-restart verification, capture sync exit codes, emit last-successful-sync heartbeat + Telegram alert, replace get_public_url.sh fixed sleeps with readiness polling, add LLM backoff. Depend on the live host and the /readiness route from slice 10.

A11Y-08 (FCC-HTML sanitization): bleach-allowlist + heading normalization of fcc_description server-side at write time so the document outline stays coherent. Best done in the write path alongside the markdown-precompute migration.

DBN-4 (unwritten tables) / SSOT-8 (admin/credential state in flat files): document concept_strengths/user_notebooks as read-only placeholders (do not drop, append-only) and either wire a writer or add a tracking task; consolidate admin identity/service keys into a canonical settings store or document the deliberate DB/file split. Design decisions, low urgency, server-adjacent.
crossCuttingRisks: Serial-edit conflict on style.css and the three big templates (home/challenges/challenge_detail): brand-tokens (slices 3-5), a11y contrast (slice 7), perf attributes (slice 8), and responsiveness (slice 9) ALL touch these same files. The ordering (tokens -> a11y -> perf -> responsiveness) is chosen so each line reaches its final form with the fewest reopens, but reviewers must apply the slices strictly in order or literals/classes will drift back in.

Visual-regression risk is concentrated in BT-5 (neutral-scale swap), BT-8/BT-9 (inline-to-class + .markdown-content reconciliation). Each rendered page (home, calendar, detail, admin, register, verify, dashboard, sql) should be eyeballed against the current Scientific American editorial look after slices 4-5; the goal is standardized plumbing, not a redesign.

The self-healing /readiness route (slice 10) and the server-only watchdog rewiring (deferred) are a split pair: pushing the route now is safe and inert, but the health-driven recovery only takes effect once the host-side watchdog is repointed in the production window. Do not consider self-healing complete until both halves land.

Append-only schema HARD RULE governs every DB-touching item: no DROP of columns/tables ever (concept_strengths, user_notebooks, quote_text, image_path NOT NULL all stay). All schema change is additive + idempotent with no-op downgrade. Model edits (SSOT-5/DBN-3) must make the model MATCH the live NOT NULL constraint, not attempt to relax it.

Auth-gating /admin (slice 11) currently relies on a server-side ADMIN_KEY compare because OTP/OAuth are stubs (S-11); this is an interim control. It must be in place BEFORE the Cloudflare tunnel is exposed, and must not be built to depend on the mock auth.

The api_challenge single-source-of-truth pattern (recently fixed) is the reference the lineage/self-healing slices build on; the JSON error envelope (SH-9) must keep /api/challenge returning parseable JSON on 5xx so the calendar modal fallback keeps working.

Secret rotation (S-1/S-2) is owner-deferred to production but the code still contains live secrets in tracked ops scripts; until SU-5 history-purge + sparse-checkout run on the host, treat the repo as compromised and do NOT widen public exposure.
plan.executiveSummary: The nine audits converge on one truth: the repo has genuine artifacts (a W3C-style token kit, an append-only schema, a working Pyodide engine, a resilient tunnel/watchdog topology) but bypasses them everywhere, and the correct fix order is dictated by file-conflict minimization, not the user's literal sequence. The dependency root is the design-token layer in style.css: petrol #005863, JetBrains Mono, and the entire Bootstrap-derived neutral palette are used but untokenized, so responsiveness (clamp() type/sizing) and the two AA contrast fixes cannot land cleanly until those tokens exist. The plan therefore front-loads app.py-only code hygiene (which conflicts with nothing and simultaneously clears the fabricated-stats and dead-loader lineage findings), then lands the CSS token foundation in three sub-slices (define -> replace literals -> extract classes), and only afterward layers accessibility, client-side performance, and mobile-first responsiveness on top of the now-canonical layer. This keeps the user's north-star intent (code -> logic -> UI -> algorithms -> DB -> lineage -> self-healing -> optimization -> ease-of-access UI -> responsiveness -> connectedness -> secured server) but reorders where dependencies demand: DB normalization and algorithm/optimization work is largely server-only (a schema-reconciling additive migration, WebP derivatives, cache headers, markdown precompute, gunicorn runtime, sudoers, secret rotation) and is deferred to the production window since the host is unreachable via VPN, while every code change reaches the server through GitHub auto-deploy. Eleven ordered, push-now slices carry the client-side and code-level work (tokens, a11y, perf-markup, responsiveness, health/observability routes, and code-side security hardening including auth-gating /admin and a fail-closed SECRET_KEY); twelve deferred server items carry everything requiring the live host or DB. The overriding constraints are the append-only schema HARD RULE (no DROP, additive/idempotent only), preserving the Scientific American editorial look (standardize plumbing, do not redesign), and applying the token/a11y/perf/responsiveness slices strictly in order because they repeatedly touch the same three templates and style.css.
